allowed: Read, profile, and audit PR 346, its prior OKRA evidence, published package surfaces, documentation, examples, tests, lint, and benchmark harnesses
Edit Lite, SDK, provider, test, documentation, example, lint, benchmark, manifest, changeset, and PR surfaces after accepted discovery
Run deterministic local gates, paired benchmarks, allocation probes, package builds, and independent review
Commit and push narrow revisions to PR 346 after active walls hold
approval_gates: Only the human may change the objective, metric thresholds, action envelope, merge decision, release decision, or standing-wall retirement
No CKR contribution or PKR delivery is accepted without an accepted source DKR checkpoint and fresh active-wall reads
Performance evidence must be replayed by an independent validator before any improvement claim
forbidden: Merge, publish, tag, release, deploy, or close PR 346
Access or mutate real GitHub issue data, production PostgreSQL, Victoria, or another production service
Add MCP, automatic tool collection, implicit built-ins, shared production scopes, pooled mutable execution contexts, or hidden dependency transport
Pass ctx or scope into inline operation callbacks
Skip dependency activation, cleanup, cancellation, extensions, tracing, tests, benchmark rows, or evidence requirements to improve a metric
Force push, destructively reset, discard user work, rewrite prior terminal evidence, or perform unrelated cleanup
action_envelope.rollback: Keep changes as narrow commits on feat/session-kernel; revert only the failing revision and preserve prior evidence

anti_goals.read_method: Keep work to the proven boundary lookup defect and its tests
Preserve the contradiction and reject stale or self-certified success
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Prior terminal record, CI failure, successor store, and final evidence

anti_goals.activation_condition: Every claim, disposition, and completion decision
anti_goals.read_method: Verify run store and reject self-certified completion claims
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Append-only OKRA records plus deterministic or independent evidence

anti_goals.activation_condition: Public provider handles or model selection changes
anti_goals.read_method: Run provider type tests and compare exported model seam
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Existing type tests and public export assertions

anti_goals.read_method: Replay close order and durable-state checks
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Structured lifetime and React cleanup tests

anti_goals.read_method: Prove sibling and nested explicit boundaries do not share owner-local resources

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any history rewrite, release, deletion, push, or tmux shutdown
anti_goals.read_method: Audit executed commands and require captured acknowledgement before stopping windows
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Command ledger, Git state, and tmux state

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any public API, docs, example, lint, skill, or runtime change
anti_goals.read_method: Replay docs/alignment/lint gates and review changed contracts against implementation
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Shipped code, public docs, alignment checks, and independent review

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any code, package, build, or configuration change
anti_goals.read_method: Run affected package and repository gates, then parse final review verdict
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Recorded gate exits and independent Claude review

anti_goals.activation_condition: Namespace, facade, flow, or exported object changes
anti_goals.read_method: Every effectful child call must resolve to a declared dependency edge
Run strengthened dependency lint and dependency-resolution conformance tests
Run valid fixtures and count unexpected diagnostics

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any integration, replay, rejection, or tmux shutdown
anti_goals.read_method: Compare each discovered unit manifest with final main and recorded disposition
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Git commit and path manifests plus tmux captures

anti_goals.read_method: Run identical lint inputs twice and compare normalized diagnostics
Inspect child exit and transport disposal after close and cancel

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any session contract, package, release, or documentation change
anti_goals.read_method: Assert no MCP/tool collection implementation or publication is included
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Changed-path and public export audit plus npm registry read

anti_goals.read_method: Require order-independent tests at both core and adapter seams
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Generic Lite and React boundary tests

anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-2 defines and funds a deterministic graph-edge checker

anti_goals.activation_condition: Definition handles or wiring types change
anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-L1 narrows mechanically provable ceremony rules
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Lint diagnostics and deterministic API-shape assertions

anti_goals.activation_condition: Provider code or lint configuration changes
anti_goals.read_method: Changed-path audit rejects provider-specific suppression additions
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Changed lint configuration and source suppressions

anti_goals.read_method: Require at least one passing and one failing fixture per new rule shape
Reject module mocks, global patches, and internal reaches
Reject new observation or context exports
Require deterministic replay and an independent reviewer before acceptance

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any source, test, doc, or OKRA change
anti_goals.read_method: Run style gates and inspect the narrow diff
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Repository lint, plain-wording check, and diff check

anti_goals.read_method: Build lint tool and scan every touched source, test, example, and doc path
Run every touched surface and require all six PR checks
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Focused suites, root lint, and PR CI

anti_goals.read_method: Allow only the approved branch push; no publication, release, merge, or production call
Run lint and inspect changed execution edges

anti_goals.activation_condition: Roots, permissions, auth, environment, or process spawning changes
anti_goals.read_method: Reducing DKR DKR-3 defines provider-specific denial probes
Compare known unsupported syntax classes with documented limitations
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Rule documentation and invalid fixture matrix

anti_goals.read_method: Important evidence must resolve by hash or governed source record

anti_goals.activation_condition: Run artifacts or product files are written
anti_goals.read_method: Important writes use the helper or carry a target plus content hash
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Move records, content hashes, and changed-path audit

anti_goals.read_method: Use the store helper for metrics, events, check-ins, and terminal records
Reducing DKR DKR-L1 determines a deterministic namespace-alignment read
Compare touched paths with ratified provider, lint, test, docs, and OKRA scope

anti_goals.activation_condition: Config, engine, transport, auth, or provider wiring changes
anti_goals.read_method: Run no-implicit-binding lint and prove missing bindings fail during dependency resolution
Keep the 16-contract verifier green if the surface is touched
Require a syntactically provable invariant for every diagnostic

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any lifecycle callback, inline operation, flow, resource, or public execution API change
anti_goals.read_method: Reject passing ctx or scope as callback arguments or runtime parameters; core-owned context access remains internal
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Lite type tests, lint fixtures, API audit, and session-kernel-ci-node24 continuation record

anti_goals.read_method: Reject mixed environments, changed workloads, source-tree imports, missing rows, optional peeking, or invalid allocation methods
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Benchmark harness validation, artifact fingerprints, workload manifest, and comparator output

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any performance conclusion, PR wording, or completion claim
anti_goals.read_method: Reject claims based on source inspection, one run, inconclusive evidence, or writer narrative
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Saved paired observations, comparator replay, allocation evidence, and independent validator record

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any Lite, SDK, provider, or benchmark implementation change
anti_goals.read_method: Require established common rows to hold the 0.95 no-regression threshold under the predeclared pair protocol
Reject a throughput win when any representative allocation ratio exceeds 1.05
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Validated allocation sampler and predeclared representative workload results

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any execution-context, session, resource, streaming, listener, or caching change
anti_goals.read_method: Reject a candidate whose retained-memory upper-bound ratio exceeds 1.05
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Calibrated retained-memory batch probe after complete close and disposal

anti_goals.read_method: Reject production branches, flags, or shortcuts that exist only to improve benchmark results

ckrs.contribution: The published major-release contract is complete, current, executable, and consistent across declarations, documentation, examples, migration, package exports, changeset, and PR text.
ckrs.delivery: Accepted gaps become narrow API, TSDoc, documentation, migration, example, and checker PKRs. CKR is measurable contribution context, not a worker job.
ckrs.discovery: DKR-C derives the published surface and the executable coverage matrix.

ckrs.contribution: The new lifecycle and session kernel use measurably less time and allocation without weakening existing behavior.
ckrs.delivery: Accepted hot paths become one-change paired-measurement PKRs. CKR is measurable contribution context, not a worker job.
ckrs.discovery: DKR-P validates the throughput, allocation, retained-memory, and workload evidence before optimization.

human_confirmation_boundary.boundary: Human owns frame and threshold changes, standing-wall retirement, merge, publication, release, deployment, and production access.

anti_goals.source_of_truth: Named deterministic checker, test, trace, benchmark artifact, changed-path audit, or governed record per wall

objective.lag_rule: Immediate after an accepted slice and at terminalization
objective.read_method: Read direct component metrics and count misses; never derive success from completed tasks
objective.source_of_truth: Built package contract checker, full release gates, paired performance comparison, and active-wall records at the exact PR head

objective.definition: Count direct public-contract gaps, failing release gates, performance target misses, and active anti-goal breaches on the exact PR head.

operating_loop.cadence: Check in on worker finish, unknown, flag, and every ten minutes while work is live.
operating_loop.introspection_cadence: DKR acceptance, any flag, level descent, terminalization, and every third check-in.
operating_loop.stale_data_policy: Refresh or fund discovery; approval cannot make stale evidence fresh.

transport.detected_via: collaboration tools exposed by the runtime
transport.model_phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.

dkrs.decision_target: Derive the exact published API and documentation coverage matrix that makes public_contract_gap_count mechanically readable.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop when every published package export and related release-content family has a source-backed coverage row, or return a replayable empty/blocked checkpoint.

dkrs.decision_target: Decide the valid throughput, allocation, and retained-memory measurement design and rank evidence-backed optimization candidates.
dkrs.stop_rule: Stop after validating or rejecting the measurement design and ranking source-backed candidates; do not optimize or sample until a result turns favorable.

orchestrator.terminal_condition: Continue until release_readiness_gap_count equals 0 with every active wall held, the human changes or stops the frame, or a blocking flag requires human resolution.

payload.cause: The v1 packet was drafted before its own governance commit and did not refresh exact_start_head after that commit.
payload.correction: Supersede only dispatch provenance with worker-packets/pkr-a1-exec-api.v2.json; product scope, target contract, thresholds, and authority are unchanged.
payload.finding: The v1 packet recorded the pre-governance commit 8af92a49 as exact_start_head, but PKR-A1 began after the governed tree and packet commit 8504cafb.
payload.impact: Validator rejected exact-head provenance while accepting functional gates.

payload.cause: The second exact-head build hit ENOSPC before any performance observation.
payload.finding: Older scratch studies were archived outside /tmp, then the exact same frozen P9 build resumed and completed before the first observation.
payload.impact: No observation was lost or repeated. The evidence proves ENOSPC and successful pre-observation recovery; it does not prove a narrower inode-specific cause.

payload.cause: The exact one-file candidate exceeded the frozen per-row allocation ceiling in managed and SDK lifecycle rows.
The known comparison completed, but the cause of the row-specific allocation regressions is a new uncertainty.
Complete sample-to-stack closure failed for four immutable profiles, so the analyzer returned no attribution instead of dropping or inventing evidence.

payload.action: Preserve the candidate source by content hash, then restore the product file to exact commit 293dcdf2 before the next counterfactual.
payload.cause: Independent performance evidence killed the full lazy-state candidate as a target-satisfying hypothesis.
The isolated V1+V2+V3 counterfactual held every safety wall but missed both geometric-mean performance targets.
The scratch candidate exceeded the frozen per-row allocation ceiling in the SDK session lifecycle row.
Independent validation accepted the SDK allocation ceiling breach, and the challenger isolated the changed explicit caller-signal branch as the smallest reducing boundary.
The hybrid scratch candidate exceeded the frozen SDK allocation ceiling.
Independent validation found that P14's semantic counts were copied into the protocol without preserved logs or a replay command.
payload.finding: The test source hash is replayable, but the claimed pass counts are not independently supported.
payload.impact: P14 semantic counts are rejected. Later candidates must preserve exact semantic commands, logs, exit codes, and hashes before performance observation.

payload.cause: P14 failed the SDK allocation ceiling and added a non-baseline bound-listener bridge. The challenger accepted inherited baseline-composition mode as the smallest reducing route.
